titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.32

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=636 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:27:40.534581+00 | translation moa=637 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:27:42.509664+00

div.p:
Ricardus dei gratia ecclesie beati Andree humilis minister vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis  salutem . Sciatis tam posteri quam presentes nos dedisse et concessisse et presentis scripti mu
nimine confirmasse deo et ecclesie Sancti Andree et canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus et seruituris . ecclesiam de hadintunia cum terra de Clerckintune per rectas di
uisas suas . et cum ceteris omnibus eidem ecclesie iuste pertinentibus . in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam tenendam libere et quiete et honorifice  ab omni exactione et consuetu
dine . salua tenura monialium quam eis dedimus et carta nostra confirmauimus . Quare uolumus et auctoritate episcopali mandamus ut predicti canonici prenominatam
ecclesiam adeo libere teneant . quiete et honorifice possideant . sicut aliqua ecclesia in tota diocesi nostra liberius tenetur . quiecius et honorificentius possidetur . saluis syno
dalibus et auxiliis episcopalibus . Hiis testibus . Iohanne abbate de Sancta Cruce . A . archidiacono Laod’ . Waltero archidiacono de Sancto Andrea . I . priore de hadingt’. Willelmo priore de Gal
weia . Willelmo priore de Sancta cruce . Roberto fratre episcopi . Iohanne de Sancto Andrea . Rogero de Listune . Roberto de pert . Alexandro et Willelmo capellanis episcopi . Galfrido
dapifero episcopi . Magistro Abraham . Stephano clerico . Willelmo filio Roberti filii Gvidonis . Adam fratre Comitis Dunk’ . Merlesuano filio merlesuani . Odone Senes
caldo . Gospatricio de Rerais . hugone pincerna . Waltero nepote episcopi . Willelmo camerario . Gvidone marescaldo . Gamel ostiario . et aliis quam pluribus boni testi
monii uiris ;

p.cl: Richard, by the grace of God a humble servant of blessed Andrew to all sons of Holy Mother Church [sends] greeting.
Know us, as much those to come after as those present,

to have given, conceded, and by the guarantee of the present writing to have made firm, to God, the church of St Andrew, and to the canons serving God in that same place now and in the future, the church of Haddington with the land of Clerkington by its right marches, and with everything else rightfully pertaining to the same church, in free and everlasting alms, to be held freely and quitly and honourably from every exaction and custom, saving the tenure of the nuns which we gave to them and affirmed by our charter. Wherefore we will and by episcopal authority command that the aforesaid canons hold the aforenamed church as freely, and quitly and honourably possess, just as any church in our whole diocese is held most freely, and quitly and honourably possessed, saving synodal dues and episcopal aids.

With these witnesses: John, abbot of Holyrood; Andrew, archdeacon of St Andrews; Ilbert, prior of Haddington; William, prior of Galloway; William, prior of Holyrood; Robert, the bishop's brother; John of St Andrews; Roger of Liston; Robert of Perth; Alexander and William, the bishop's chaplains; Geoffrey, the bishop's steward; Master Abraham; Stephen the clerk; William, son of Robert son of Guy; Adam, brother of Earl Duncan; Merlswain son of Merlswain; Odo the steward; Cospatric of Rires; Hugh the butler; Walter the bishop's nephew; William the chamberlain; Guy the marischal; Gamel the doorward; and very many other men of good character.
